title: noExcessiveLinesPerFile
description: Learn more about noExcessiveLinesPerFile

import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

<Tabs>
<TabItem label="CSS" icon="seti:css">
## Summary
- Rule available since: `v2.3.12`
- Diagnostic Category: [`lint/style/noExcessiveLinesPerFile`](/reference/diagnostics#diagnostic-category)
- This rule isn't recommended, so you need to enable it.
- This rule doesn't have a fix.
- The default severity of this rule is [**information**](/reference/diagnostics#information).
## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": "error"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
Restrict the number of lines in a file.

Large files tend to do many things and can make it hard to follow what's going on.
This rule can help enforce a limit on the number of lines in a file.

## Examples

### Invalid

The following example will show a diagnostic when `maxLines` is set to 2:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 2
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```css
.a { color: red; }
.b { color: blue; }
.c { color: green; }
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.css:1:1 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-excessive-lines-per-file">lint/style/noExcessiveLinesPerFile</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This file has too many lines (3). Maximum allowed is 2.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>1 │ </strong>.a &#123; color: red; &#125;<br />   <strong>   │ </strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>2 │ </strong>.b &#123; color: blue; &#125;<br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>3 │ </strong>.c &#123; color: green; &#125;<br />   <strong>   │ </strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>4 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Consider splitting this file into smaller files.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

### Valid

```css
.a { color: red; }
.b { color: blue; }
```

## Options

### `maxLines`

This option sets the maximum number of lines allowed in a file.
If the file exceeds this limit, a diagnostic will be reported.

Default: `300`

#### Examples

The default value for `maxLines` is `300`. The following example shows how to set the
`maxLines` option to a smaller value. It reports a diagnostic because the file has more
than 4 lines:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 4
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```css
.a { color: red; }
.b { color: blue; }
.c { color: green; }
.d { color: yellow; }
.e { color: purple; }
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.css:1:1 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-excessive-lines-per-file">lint/style/noExcessiveLinesPerFile</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This file has too many lines (5). Maximum allowed is 4.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>1 │ </strong>.a &#123; color: red; &#125;<br />   <strong>   │ </strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>2 │ </strong>.b &#123; color: blue; &#125;<br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>3 │ </strong>.c &#123; color: green; &#125;<br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>4 │ </strong>.d &#123; color: yellow; &#125;<br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>5 │ </strong>.e &#123; color: purple; &#125;<br />   <strong>   │ </strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>6 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Consider splitting this file into smaller files.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

### `skipBlankLines`

When this option is set to `true`, blank lines are not counted towards the maximum line limit.

Default: `false`

#### Examples

The following example shows how `skipBlankLines` can prevent a diagnostic by excluding blank
lines from the total count:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 2,
						"skipBlankLines": true
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```css
.a { color: red; }


.b { color: blue; }
```

## Suppressions

If you need to exceed the line limit in a specific file, you can suppress this rule
at the top of the file:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 2
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```css
/* biome-ignore-all lint/style/noExcessiveLinesPerFile: generated file */
.a { color: red; }
.b { color: blue; }
.c { color: green; }
```

- [Disable a rule](/linter/#disable-a-rule)

<TabItem label="GraphQL" icon="seti:graphql">
## Summary
- Rule available since: `v2.3.12`
- Diagnostic Category: [`lint/style/noExcessiveLinesPerFile`](/reference/diagnostics#diagnostic-category)
- This rule isn't recommended, so you need to enable it.
- This rule doesn't have a fix.
- The default severity of this rule is [**information**](/reference/diagnostics#information).
## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": "error"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
Restrict the number of lines in a file.

Large files tend to do many things and can make it hard to follow what's going on.
This rule can help enforce a limit on the number of lines in a file.

## Examples

### Invalid

The following example will show a diagnostic when `maxLines` is set to 2:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 2
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```graphql
query Foo { id }
query Bar { id }
query Baz { id }
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text"></code></pre>

### Valid

```graphql
query Foo { id }
query Bar { id }
```

## Options

### `maxLines`

This option sets the maximum number of lines allowed in a file.
If the file exceeds this limit, a diagnostic will be reported.

Default: `300`

#### Examples

The default value for `maxLines` is `300`. The following example shows how to set the
`maxLines` option to a smaller value. It reports a diagnostic because the file has more
than 4 lines:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 4
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```graphql
query Foo { id }
query Bar { id }
query Baz { id }
query Qux { id }
query Quux { id }
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text"></code></pre>

### `skipBlankLines`

When this option is set to `true`, blank lines are not counted towards the maximum line limit.

Default: `false`

#### Examples

The following example shows how `skipBlankLines` can prevent a diagnostic by excluding blank
lines from the total count:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 2,
						"skipBlankLines": true
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```graphql
query Foo { id }


query Bar { id }
```

## Suppressions

If you need to exceed the line limit in a specific file, you can suppress this rule
at the top of the file:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 2
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```graphql
query Foo { id }
query Bar { id }
query Baz { id }
```

<TabItem label="JavaScript (and super languages)" icon="seti:javascript">
## Summary
- Rule available since: `v2.3.12`
- Diagnostic Category: [`lint/style/noExcessiveLinesPerFile`](/reference/diagnostics#diagnostic-category)
- This rule isn't recommended, so you need to enable it.
- This rule doesn't have a fix.
- The default severity of this rule is [**information**](/reference/diagnostics#information).
- Sources: 
  - Inspired from [`max-lines`](https://eslint.org/docs/latest/rules/max-lines)

## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": "error"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
Restrict the number of lines in a file.

Large files tend to do many things and can make it hard to follow what's going on.
This rule can help enforce a limit on the number of lines in a file.

## Examples

### Invalid

The following example will show a diagnostic when `maxLines` is set to 2:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 2
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```js
const a = 1;
const b = 2;
const c = 3;
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.js:1:1 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-excessive-lines-per-file">lint/style/noExcessiveLinesPerFile</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This file has too many lines (3). ### Valid

```js
const a = 1;
const b = 2;
```

## Options

### `maxLines`

This option sets the maximum number of lines allowed in a file.
If the file exceeds this limit, a diagnostic will be reported.

Default: `300`

#### Examples

The default value for `maxLines` is `300`. The following example shows how to set the
`maxLines` option to a smaller value. It reports a diagnostic because the file has more
than 4 lines:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 4
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```js
const a = 1;
const b = 2;
const c = 3;
const d = 4;
const e = 5;
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.js:1:1 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-excessive-lines-per-file">lint/style/noExcessiveLinesPerFile</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This file has too many lines (5). style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Consider splitting this file into smaller files.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

### `skipBlankLines`

When this option is set to `true`, blank lines are not counted towards the maximum line limit.

Default: `false`

#### Examples

The following example shows how `skipBlankLines` can prevent a diagnostic by excluding blank
lines from the total count:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 2,
						"skipBlankLines": true
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```js
const a = 1;


const b = 2;
```

## Suppressions

If you need to exceed the line limit in a specific file, you can suppress this rule
at the top of the file:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 2
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```js
// biome-ignore-all lint/style/noExcessiveLinesPerFile: generated file
const a = 1;
const b = 2;
const c = 3;
```

:::note
This rule has been implemented but not released yet. It will be available in the next release.
:::
## Summary
- Diagnostic Category: [`lint/style/noExcessiveLinesPerFile`](/reference/diagnostics#diagnostic-category)
- This rule isn't recommended, so you need to enable it.
- This rule doesn't have a fix.
- The default severity of this rule is [**information**](/reference/diagnostics#information).
## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": "error"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
Restrict the number of lines in a file.

Large files tend to do many things and can make it hard to follow what's going on.
This rule can help enforce a limit on the number of lines in a file.

## Examples

### Invalid

The following example will show a diagnostic when `maxLines` is set to 2:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 2
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```html
<div></div>
<span></span>
<p></p>
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text"></code></pre>

### Valid

```html
<div></div>
<span></span>
```

## Options

### `maxLines`

This option sets the maximum number of lines allowed in a file.
If the file exceeds this limit, a diagnostic will be reported.

Default: `300`

#### Examples

The default value for `maxLines` is `300`. The following example shows how to set the
`maxLines` option to a smaller value. It reports a diagnostic because the file has more
than 4 lines:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 4
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```html
<div>Line 1</div>
<div>Line 2</div>
<div>Line 3</div>
<div>Line 4</div>
<div>Line 5</div>
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text"></code></pre>

### `skipBlankLines`

When this option is set to `true`, blank lines are not counted towards the maximum line limit.

Default: `false`

#### Examples

The following example shows how `skipBlankLines` can prevent a diagnostic by excluding blank
lines from the total count:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 2,
						"skipBlankLines": true
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```html
<div></div>


<span></span>
```

## Suppressions

If you need to exceed the line limit in a specific file, you can suppress this rule
at the top of the file:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"style": {
				"noExcessiveLinesPerFile": {
					"options": {
						"maxLines": 2
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```html
<!-- biome-ignore-all lint/style/noExcessiveLinesPerFile: generated file -->
<div></div>
<span></span>
<p></p>
```
